title: Stripe, Anthropic, and OpenAI back a $500M respiratory-infection effort
friendlyTitle: AI and tech funders are backing a big push to stop colds and flu
summary: MIT Technology Review reports that Stripe, Anthropic, the OpenAI Foundation, Bill Gates, Flu Lab, and others are backing Intercept, a $500 million nonprofit aimed at preventing common respiratory infections. The effort plans to fund broad vaccines, RNA drugs, antibodies, engineered nasal-spray proteins, and air-cleaning systems for shared spaces.
friendlySummary: A new nonprofit called Intercept is raising $500 million to support better ways to prevent respiratory infections, from broad vaccines to cleaner indoor air.
whyItMatters: This is a reminder that AI-era fortunes are increasingly funding public-health infrastructure and computational biology, not only software products. If successful, the daily-life impact would be very direct: fewer colds and flu infections in schools, offices, and public spaces.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This could matter to almost everyone because colds and flu interrupt daily life. Less sick time would be a very practical kind of innovation.
